rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (82-86 Pond St, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for any sale confirmation; however, no reappraisal for sale has been filed yet, and the buyer does not appear to be a viable LLC. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILY C. TIMLOW TRUST FBO RICHARD CRAIG HILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Co-Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Another amended petition was filed on 10/29/24, when it is actually the SECOND amended petition. (2) The Notice of Hearing must "specify [the] complete title" of the petition. (3) Caron Schmierer was only just appointed as the guardian ad litem for the beneficiary on 11/20/24. (4) Under what authority was the modification to the trust executed in 2005? Craig's guardian ad litem at that time, K. Bruce Friedman, did not even sign that modification "made non-judicially" and without any such authority provided for such undertaking in the trust. Please provide courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The two paragraphs creating the trusts for Gordon Kent Hill and Richard Craig Hill were paragraphs B.1 and B.2.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDITH C. YATES LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2019 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The two trust beneficiaries are minors. No guardian ad litem has been appointed to represent their interests. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The ex-spouse is presumably the adult who has legal custody of the minors, but neither the petition nor the service list clearly indicated that he was entitled to notice as such custodian. [CRC 7.51(d).] (4) A copy of each financial institution statement immediately preceding the settlor's date of death must be filed to show how title to the accounts was vested. (5) The proposed order has different accounts from the accounts listed on Exhibit C. (6) What is the successor trustee's address? (There is no Article Twelve in the trust.) Please provide a revised proposed order, if applicable, and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/8/25. NB: Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Please do not provide copies of unfiled papers. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE PUI FONG QUAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Deadline for providing a courtesy copy to the assigned examiner is 12/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 6 of a petition for probate should not be completed when a decedent is survived by issue.) NB: Lack of courtesy copies can delay review. No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]
